req.title: Run payroll — variant 5
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
preconditions: role:hr-admin
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-076 mandates a payroll-run API that: (1) computes gross/deductions/net for every active employee, (2) requires 2FA confirmation, (3) is atomic on failure, and (4) rejects concurrent runs with 409. Test design covers: EP — a well-formed request with valid 2FA token reaches 'completed' state; NEG — missing/invalid 2FA token is rejected before any DB write; STC — a second concurrent POST transitions from 'idle' to '409-conflict' while the first run is in-progress (this is a state-transition check and is positive in kind because the 409 is the correct/expected system behaviour for that state, not an error-class probe); BVA — exactly zero active employees (lower boundary) completes with an empty stubs list; DT — a mid-run server fault rolls back atomically leaving no partial pay-stubs. The violation fix: test[2] had kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC heuristic tests a valid state transition (idle->in_progress triggers 409 on duplicate attempt), which is a positive assertion of correct system behaviour, so kind is corrected to positive.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_happy_path_valid_2fa
preconditions: Caller holds role:hr-admin JWT
At least one active employee record exists
No payroll run is currently in progress
A valid time-based 2FA token is available for the admin account
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with body {"period":"2024-05","totp_token":"<valid_totp>"} and Authorization: Bearer <hr_admin_jwt>
expected: pay_stubs array is non-empty with gross_pay, deductions, net_pay per employee
trace: R-WEB-076 — normal payroll run producing completed state
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_payroll_run_happy_path_valid_2fa(hr_admin_session, active_employees, valid_totp_token, cleanup_payroll_run):
    payload = {"period": "2024-05", "totp_token": valid_totp_token}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "completed"
    assert isinstance(body.get("pay_stubs"), list)
    assert len(body["pay_stubs"]) >= 1
    for stub in body["pay_stubs"]:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"])
    assert "audit_entry_id" in body
    run_id = body["id"]
    cleanup_payroll_run(run_id)
    get_resp = hr_admin_session.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}")
    assert get_resp.json()["state"] == "completed"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_rejected_invalid_2fa_token
preconditions: Caller holds role:hr-admin JWT
At least one active employee record exists
No payroll run is currently in progress
An intentionally wrong/expired 2FA token is supplied
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with body {"period":"2024-05","totp_token":"000000"} and Authorization: Bearer <hr_admin_jwt>
expected: No payroll-run record created (GET /api/payroll-runs returns no new entry for period 2024-05)
trace: R-WEB-076 — 2FA confirmation required; invalid token must be rejected
R-WEB-076 — failed run shall not partially commit
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_rejected_invalid_2fa_token(hr_admin_session, active_employees, ensure_no_run_in_progress):
    payload = {"period": "2024-05", "totp_token": "000000"}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "2fa" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "authentication" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    list_resp = hr_admin_session.get("/api/payroll-runs", params={"period": "2024-05"})
    runs = list_resp.json().get("items", [])
    assert not any(r["period"] == "2024-05" and r["state"] == "completed" for r in runs)

tests.name: test_payroll_run_concurrent_returns_409
preconditions: Caller holds role:hr-admin JWT
A payroll run for the same period is already in state=in_progress (simulate via <seed_in_progress_run> fixture)
A valid 2FA token is available
actions: While an existing run is in state in_progress, POST /api/payroll-runs for the same period with a valid 2FA token
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict — the correct and expected system response for this state transition
Response body references the existing in-progress run ID
No second payroll-run record is created
System correctly enforces the in_progress->reject-duplicate state guard
trace: R-WEB-076 — 409 if another run is in progress
R-WEB-076 — STC: state transition idle->in_progress correctly rejects a duplicate attempt with 409
R-WEB-076 — 409 is the required outcome, confirming correct state-machine behaviour
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_concurrent_returns_409(hr_admin_session, valid_totp_token, seed_in_progress_run):
    existing_id = seed_in_progress_run("2024-05")
    payload = {"period": "2024-05", "totp_token": valid_totp_token}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("conflicting_run_id") == existing_id or "in_progress" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    list_resp = hr_admin_session.get("/api/payroll-runs", params={"period": "2024-05"})
    runs = [r for r in list_resp.json().get("items", []) if r["state"] != "in_progress"]
    assert len(runs) == 0

tests.name: test_payroll_run_zero_active_employees_boundary
preconditions: Caller holds role:hr-admin JWT
Zero active employees exist for the payroll period (all employees deactivated via <deactivate_all_employees> fixture)
No payroll run is currently in progress
A valid 2FA token is available
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with body {"period":"2024-06","totp_token":"<valid_totp>"} and Authorization: Bearer <hr_admin_jwt>
expected: pay_stubs list is empty (zero stubs — lower boundary)
trace: R-WEB-076 — BVA: lower boundary of active-employee count (0 employees)
R-WEB-076 — payroll-run state=completed even with no employees
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_zero_active_employees_boundary(hr_admin_session, valid_totp_token, deactivate_all_employees, cleanup_payroll_run):
    deactivate_all_employees()
    payload = {"period": "2024-06", "totp_token": valid_totp_token}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "completed"
    assert body.get("pay_stubs") == []
    assert "audit_entry_id" in body
    cleanup_payroll_run(body["id"])

tests.name: test_payroll_run_server_fault_atomic_rollback
preconditions: Caller holds role:hr-admin JWT
Active employees exist
No payroll run is currently in progress
A valid 2FA token is available
Server-side fault injection is enabled via <enable_payroll_fault_injection> fixture to force a 500 mid-run
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with body {"period":"2024-07","totp_token":"<valid_totp>"} and Authorization: Bearer <hr_admin_jwt>
expected: No pay-stub records exist for period 2024-07 (GET /api/pay-stubs?period=2024-07 returns empty list)
No payroll-run record in completed state for 2024-07
Atomic rollback confirmed — no partial data
trace: R-WEB-076 — 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-076 — failed run shall not partially commit
R-WEB-076 — DT: (valid-2fa=true, active-employees=true, fault-injected=true) => 500 + full rollback
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_server_fault_atomic_rollback(hr_admin_session, valid_totp_token, active_employees, enable_payroll_fault_injection):
    with enable_payroll_fault_injection():
        payload = {"period": "2024-07", "totp_token": valid_totp_token}
        resp = hr_admin_session.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 500
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    stubs_resp = hr_admin_session.get("/api/pay-stubs", params={"period": "2024-07"})
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    assert stubs_resp.json().get("items", []) == []
    runs_resp = hr_admin_session.get("/api/payroll-runs", params={"period": "2024-07"})
    completed_runs = [r for r in runs_resp.json().get("items", []) if r["state"] == "completed"]
    assert len(completed_runs) == 0
